places.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
places.description: A bustling public market offering a massive array of fresh seafood, sushi stalls, and Japanese kitchen tools.
places.tips: Most shops close by early afternoon; visit between 8:00 AM and 11:00 AM for the best selection.

places.name: Shinjuku Omoide Yokocho
places.description: A narrow alleyway filled with small yakitori stalls and izakayas, preserving a post-war Showa era atmosphere.
places.tips: Seating is very tight and many stalls have a small cover charge; bring cash as many vendors do not take cards.

places.name: Asakusa Nakamise-dori
places.description: The historic approach to Senso-ji Temple lined with dozens of stalls selling traditional Japanese snacks and sweets.
places.tips: Try the 'Age-manju' (deep-fried bean cakes); note that eating while walking is generally discouraged in Japan—stand near the stall to eat.

places.name: Ginza Kagari Main Branch
places.description: A Michelin-recognized ramen shop famous for its rich, creamy 'Tori Paitan' (chicken white broth) ramen.
places.tips: The line moves slowly due to limited seating; try to arrive 20-30 minutes before opening to minimize wait time.

places.name: Shibuya Depachika (Tokyu Foodshow)
places.description: An underground 'food department store' featuring high-end bento boxes, delicate pastries, and international delicacies.
places.tips: Visit in the evening (after 7 PM) to find significant discounts on fresh bento boxes and sushi platters.

summary: Tokyo is a premier global culinary destination that seamlessly blends centuries-old traditions with modern innovation. From high-end Michelin-starred sushi in Ginza to vibrant street food in Asakusa and smoky izakayas in Shinjuku, the city offers an unparalleled variety of flavors and dining experiences for every budget.
